title: V-Client
description: My own personal performance, visual, and quality of life mod choices. Pre configured with mostly good defaults and sane keybinds, and built on the rock solid foundation of Simply Optimized.
body:
![3D Render of the V-Client logo as it appears on the Minecraft title screen.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6ccacad508d4b1616514a8c9b29154b773565f12.png)V-Client is a vanilla-enhancing client modpack intended for use on vanilla Minecraft servers. There are many like it but this one is mine.

This pack is based on the work of [Simply Optimized](https://modrinth.com/modpack/sop) so it should boast some significant performance gains compared to vanilla.

It also contains some utility mods that I personally find convenient to have while playing vanilla minecraft, including EMI configured to act as an improved recipe book, Mouse Wheelie for inventory sorting, and ESM for muting specific sounds in game. I have personally configured the settings and keybinds for all of them to make this pack work as seamlessly as possible.

This is my first attempt at ever making a modpack for the public, so I hope you enjoy.


**WARNING**: It's not suggested to use this modpack on servers that enforce some form of anti-cheat, as mods like Spyglass Improvements, EMI, and Mouse Wheelie rapidly simulate inventory movement to fulfill their functions. Make sure to check a server's rules before using V-Client or any other gameplay affecting mod/modpack.

## Useful Keybinds
### General
```F4``` Open Coordinates Hud

```F7``` Toggle light level overlay

```Shift+F7``` Configure light level overlay

### Map
```M``` Open world map

```N``` Waypoints

```B``` New Waypoint

```Alt + M``` Toggle minimap

```Alt + N``` Toggle waypoint visibility

```Alt + B``` Instantly create temporary waypoint

### Schematics
```L``` Open Litematica menu (If you're familiar with Litematica all default "M" key combos have been swapped with "L" to avoid overlap with the map and cause it just makes more sense tbh)

```L+T``` Toggle Litematica tool mode (off by default)

```L+R``` Toggle Litematica rendering (also off by default)

### Preventer
```I``` Open Preventer config

```R``` Hold to temporarily override all currently active Preventer modules
